title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
blurb: A country neighbourhood, a winter in town, and one scandalous dash from Brighton.

title: Jane Eyre
author: Charlotte Brontë
blurb: An orphan, a charity school, a governess's post at a house with a secret - and a destitute walk across the moor.

title: The Thirty-Nine Steps
author: John Buchan
blurb: A dead man's secret, a milkman's disguise, and one hunted man run the length of Britain - Galloway moor to a chalk stair on the Channel, on the eve of the war.

title: My Ántonia
author: Willa Cather
blurb: An orphan boy from Virginia and the daughter of Bohemian immigrants grow up a mile apart on the raw Nebraska prairie. He leaves it for the university and the East and never quite feels at home again; she stays, is ruined and rises, and roots herself into the land as its truest child. A whole life measured against one stretch of red grass.

title: Don Quixote
author: Miguel de Cervantes
blurb: A half-mad old gentleman of La Mancha, his head turned by books of chivalry, rides out with the squire Sancho Panza to right wrongs that aren't there - charging windmills he swears are giants, storming inns he takes for castles. Three sallies across Spain, from the plains to the beach at Barcelona, where the world at last makes him see it as it is.

title: Heart of Darkness
author: Joseph Conrad
blurb: A steamer up the Congo, after a Company agent gone silent - and the hollow man at the end of the river.

title: David Copperfield
author: Charles Dickens
blurb: A whole life - a Suffolk churchyard, the road to Dover, and the far side of the world.

title: Great Expectations
author: Charles Dickens
blurb: A blacksmith’s boy on the Kent marshes helps a hunted convict, is summoned to a great decaying house, and comes into a secret fortune that carries him to London to be a gentleman. He is sure it flows from the cold Miss Havisham - but the money runs the other way, up from the marsh and the convict he was taught to despise.

title: Bleak House
author: Charles Dickens
blurb: Fog, Chancery, and a secret that drags a great lady from a Lincolnshire seat to a pauper's grave.

title: The Hound of the Baskervilles
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
blurb: A family curse, a spectral hound, and Holmes and Watson on the fog-bound Dartmoor moor.

title: The Lost World
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
blurb: A reporter, a raging genius, a sceptic and a hunter go up the Amazon to a plateau where the age of dinosaurs never ended - and the bridge is toppled behind them.

title: The Count of Monte Cristo
author: Alexandre Dumas
blurb: Buried without trial in the Château d'If, an innocent sailor escapes with a hidden fortune and returns - as the Count of Monte Cristo - to ruin the three men who destroyed him. Marseille to Rome to Paris, and east across the sea.

title: Madame Bovary
author: Gustave Flaubert
blurb: A farmer's daughter, fed on romantic novels, dreams of Paris and passion and never gets further than Rouen - a whole life, and its ruin, inside forty miles of Normandy.

title: Jude the Obscure
author: Thomas Hardy
blurb: A village stonemason burns to enter Christminster, the university city that bars him for his class. He carves its colleges and is never let inside. His forbidden love for his cousin Sue, and the marriages that cage them both, end in a horror that breaks them - and he dies within sound of the city’s bells, still outside the walls.

title: Tess of the d'Urbervilles
author: Thomas Hardy
blurb: One woman walking one county, renamed but barely disguised.

title: The Devil's Elixir
author: E. T. A. Hoffmann
blurb: A monk drinks a relic-wine that unchains every buried desire, and is driven from his cloister into seduction, murder and madness - dogged the whole way by his own double, a face he cannot tell from his own. Rome to Prussia, and a family curse running under all of it.

title: Les Misérables
author: Victor Hugo
blurb: A convict remade by one act of mercy, and his road across France to the Paris barricade - hunted every mile.

title: Ulysses
author: James Joyce
blurb: A single Dublin day from breakfast to the small hours - one wandering ad-man, one grieving poet forever just missing him on the same streets, and Molly awake on Eccles Street.

title: Kim
author: Rudyard Kipling
blurb: A bazaar orphan and an old lama walk the Grand Trunk Road together - the boy's search for a soldier's Red Bull, the lama's for a holy river.

title: The Wonderful Adventures of Nils
author: Selma Lagerlöf
blurb: A boy shrunk to thumb-size is carried the length of Sweden on the back of a wild goose - Skåne to Lapland and home, and made kind by the journey.

title: The Call of the Wild
author: Jack London
blurb: A ranch dog stolen from sunlit California to the Klondike traces - and the long road back into a wolf.

title: Moby-Dick
author: Herman Melville
blurb: Ishmael ships aboard a Nantucket whaler under Captain Ahab, who has bent the whole three-year voyage to one purpose: to kill the white whale that took his leg. One man's obsession drawn across three oceans, with the whale itself a rumour until it surfaces at last on the equator - for the three-day chase that sinks them all, and leaves one man to tell it.

title: Henry IV, Parts I & II
author: William Shakespeare
blurb: A truant prince, a fat knight, and a crown in doubt - from the Boar's Head tavern to the field at Shrewsbury, and a rebellion carried the length of England.

title: Frankenstein
author: Mary Shelley
blurb: A student, the thing he makes, and a pursuit that runs from the Alps to the Arctic ice.

title: Kidnapped
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
blurb: A Lowland lad kidnapped, wrecked off Mull, and driven the length of the Highlands in the flight in the heather.

title: Dracula
author: Bram Stoker
blurb: A solicitor's clerk, a ghost ship, and a chase across Europe.

title: Vanity Fair
author: William Makepeace Thackeray
blurb: Two girls leave school and part at the gate - clever, penniless Becky Sharp climbing to Mayfair and a scandal, gentle Amelia Sedley ruined and widowed at Waterloo. London, the battlefield, and the Continent, and a whole world in it.

title: War and Peace
author: Leo Tolstoy
blurb: Five lives and an army: the Petersburg salons and the country estates, and then Napoleon's half-million pouring over the Niemen to Moscow - and crawling back a thread.

title: Anna Karenina
author: Leo Tolstoy
blurb: Two loves running opposite ways down one dead-straight Russian railway - and the train at the end of it.

title: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn
author: Mark Twain
blurb: A runaway boy and an escaped enslaved man ride a raft down the Mississippi, bound - they hope - for freedom.

title: Around the World in Eighty Days
author: Jules Verne
blurb: A wager at a London club, eighty days, and the whole turning world - by steamer, rail, elephant and sledge.

title: The War of the Worlds
author: H. G. Wells
blurb: A cylinder falls on a Surrey common and the Martians turn a heat-ray on England - one man's flight across the Thames into a dead London, his brother's escape to the sea, and the tripods' march on the city.

title: Mrs Dalloway
author: Virginia Woolf
blurb: One June day in London - a party, a lost soldier, and two lives that cross the same streets without ever meeting.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
blurb: A poor boy remakes himself into Jay Gatsby and buys a mansion on Long Island for one reason: it stands across the bay from the woman he lost. All summer he throws vast parties as a lure, reaching for a green light at the end of her dock. It is a few hundred yards of water, and the whole distance between what he is and what he dreams - and it kills him.

title: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
blurb: A starving ex-student in St Petersburg talks himself into a theory - that an extraordinary man may step over any law - and murders a pawnbroker with an axe to prove he is one. The killing traps him: for the length of the book he cannot leave the few hundred yards of the Hay Market where he did it, circling the scene, the square, and the girl who will not let him lie - until confession sends him two thousand miles east.

title: Persuasion
author: Jane Austen
blurb: Eight years after she let herself be persuaded to give up a penniless sailor, Anne Elliot watches him return from the war a rich man. As her spendthrift father lets the family seat to the very navy the Elliots look down on, Anne is carried from the cold country house to the sea at Lyme, and on to Bath, and a slow, aching second chance at the love she was talked out of.

title: Sentimental Education
author: Gustave Flaubert
blurb: A young man sees a woman on a Seine steamer and falls hopelessly in love. Over twenty-five years he drifts between Paris and his provincial home, and between three women he is half-hearted about, achieving nothing - while the Revolution of 1848 convulses the streets around him and he barely notices. Flaubert’s great anti-romance, the Paris counterpart to Madame Bovary’s provincial trap.

title: The Book of Khalid
author: Ameen Rihani
blurb: The first novel in English by an Arab writer, illustrated by Kahlil Gibran. A donkey-boy from the ruins of Baalbek and his poet friend sail steerage to New York, peddle false Holy-Land trinkets from a flooding cellar, and come home changed - Khalid to preach a gospel of East and West that gets him excommunicated, gaoled and stoned, before he walks into the Egyptian desert and is gone.
